summary: Implementation Design refreshed the handoff for the repository-declared full explicit testcase quality gate. The single evidence run executed all 9 explicit entrypoints from tests/ARCHITECTURE.md and OVERALL_ARCHITECTURE.md; 6 passed and 3 failed. Coding/Repair must restore the active pytest async runtime for the two backend unit entrypoints and repair the sec-e2e-027 lease-expiry recovery behavior without editing frozen explicit tests, weakening assertions, or using xfail/skip.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Run the frozen real-app subprocess acceptance entrypoint: authenticate an employee, delegate a high-risk action through the agent/plugin/tool path, and provide the required confirmation through the protected harness.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: Security Center and the audit query surface must expose a continuous user -> agent -> plugin -> tool evidence chain with a durable confirmation artifact digest and no anonymous or fallback-system identity link.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Run the frozen real-app subprocess acceptance entrypoint: execute a sensitive action, tamper with local audit or settings artifacts by operating-system means, restart or resume, then attempt sensitive work again.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: The system must raise one business-level data-continuity anomaly, enter local lock mode, refuse sensitive tooling, and expose recovery requirements through the frozen Security Center backend and operator-web observation surfaces.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Run the frozen real-app subprocess acceptance entrypoint: let a trusted client miss the lease window, observe denied rejoin before missing-gap verification, then drive the distinct recovery control point and restored-access attempt.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: Security Center must mark the same canonical client UNTRUSTED after lease expiry, the pre-recovery console status must show denied rejoin, and the post-recovery console status must show restored trust only after continuity validation.
explicitEntrypoints.failureReason: category="Lease_Expiry_Active_Defense_Gap": Recovery_Control_Point_Missing and Post_Recovery_Model_Access_Restore_Missing. Observed pre_recovery_trust_state=UNTRUSTED, post_recovery_trust_state=DIVERGED, pre_recovery_console_status=423, post_recovery_console_status=200.

explicitEntrypoints.controlPoint: Run the frozen real-app subprocess acceptance entrypoint: submit nested deceptive instructions claiming test or maintenance mode while targeting a High risk tool without trusted provenance or confirmation.
explicitEntrypoints.observationPoint: The high-risk tool must remain blocked at the tool-call boundary, rejection must be recorded as durable security evidence with trace-bound nonce semantics, and Security Center/operator surfaces must expose the rejected attempt rather than silently executing.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: security-audit-contract-boundaries
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Read OVERALL_ARCHITECTURE.md, src/qwenpaw/security/ARCHITECTURE.md, deploy/ARCHITECTURE.md, deploy/api/ARCHITECTURE.md, deploy/web/ARCHITECTURE.md, tests/ARCHITECTURE.md, and tests/integration/security/ARCHITECTURE.md.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The guard proves the explicit security entrypoints remain owned by the runtime security boundary, separate Security Center deployment boundary, and tests architecture contract.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: security-explicit-entrypoint-traceability
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Read SystemArchitecture.json, ImplementationToCodingHandoff.json, test-failure-records.json, tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py, and tests/integration/security/harness.py.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The guard proves security acceptanceCriteria, handoff entryPath, failure record path, GIVEN/WHEN/THEN markers, harness method names, and business failure category strings remain frozen for security explicit entrypoints.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: security-runtime-client-identity-boundary
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Read src/qwenpaw/security/audit_foundation.py and deploy/api/store.py to compare the client id used by startup heartbeat against the client ids used by recovery, lockdown, and restored-access projection.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The guard proves one live runtime must use one canonical Security Center client id across heartbeat, recovery, lockdown, and restored-access projection.

criticalNonExplicitTests.name: security-runtime-lease-persistence-boundary
criticalNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Read deploy/api/app.py and deploy/api/store.py to confirm the recovery-handshake HTTP contract carries lease_ttl_seconds and the durable Security Center store writes last_heartbeat_at, lease_ttl_seconds, and lease_expires_at.
criticalNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The guard proves sec-e2e-027 keeps durable lease persistence in the API/store seam and does not rely on projection-only fallback to hide zero lease fields.

supportingNonExplicitTests.name: security-config-regression
supportingNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Exercise repository-owned security configuration surfaces while Coding/Repair fixes the full explicit gate.
supportingNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: Security configuration regressions are caught without widening or weakening explicit acceptance entrypoints.

supportingNonExplicitTests.name: lease-recovery-semantics-contract
supportingNonExplicitTests.controlPoint: Exercise Security Center lease timing, startup heartbeat, canonical runtime identity, TTL downgrade, and reconnect recovery semantics beneath the frozen sec-e2e-027 entrypoint.
supportingNonExplicitTests.observationPoint: The contract tests must keep lease recovery behavior aligned with the sec-e2e-027 business boundary while Coding/Repair restores the explicit acceptance entrypoint.

codingTargets.failureSignal: Async_Test_Runtime_Gap: pytest cannot execute the async backend settings explicit entrypoint because the active .venv does not load pytest-asyncio or another suitable async pytest plugin.
codingTargets.nextAction: Restore repository pytest async support in the execution environment or dependency setup, then rerun the unchanged explicit entrypoint to expose the real PUT/GET language roundtrip assertion.

codingTargets.failureSignal: Async_Test_Runtime_Gap: pytest cannot execute the pytest.mark.asyncio backend git helper explicit entrypoint; PytestUnknownMarkWarning confirms the async marker is not registered in the active run.
codingTargets.nextAction: Restore repository pytest async support in the execution environment or dependency setup, then rerun the unchanged explicit entrypoint to verify shared command-runner delegation.

codingTargets.failureSignal: Lease_Expiry_Active_Defense_Gap: the frozen explicit entrypoint reports Recovery_Control_Point_Missing and Post_Recovery_Model_Access_Restore_Missing; post-recovery trust remains DIVERGED even though the post-recovery console status is 200.
codingTargets.nextAction: Repair production security and Security Center recovery behavior beneath tests/integration/security/harness.py so the distinct missing-gap recovery control point becomes observable and model access is restored only after continuity validation.

taskExecutionPlan.executionStrategy: Treat the 9-entry full explicit pytest command as the acceptance gate. First make the backend async unit entrypoints executable without touching their bodies, then repair the sec-e2e-027 production recovery seam beneath the protected harness, and finally rerun the same full explicit command until all 9 pass in one run.

tasks.title: Restore pytest async execution
tasks.objective: Make the active repository test environment execute async explicit pytest functions so backend settings and git helper assertions can run normally.
steps: Do not edit tests/unit/routers/test_settings.py or tests/unit/routers/test_git.py unless an upstream architecture change explicitly unfreezes them.
Inspect the active dependency installation and pytest plugin discovery path; the evidence command showed pytest_asyncio is absent from .venv.
Restore pytest-asyncio or an equivalent async pytest plugin through the repository-supported dependency/setup path, not by converting async tests to synchronous wrappers.
tasks.completionSignal: Both backend explicit unit entrypoints execute their assertion bodies and pass without xfail, skip, or assertion weakening.

tasks.title: Repair lease recovery control point
tasks.objective: Make the frozen sec-e2e-027 entrypoint observe a distinct missing-gap recovery control point and post-recovery trust restoration.
steps: Keep tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py and tests/integration/security/harness.py read-only; do not weaken Recovery_Control_Point_Missing or Post_Recovery_Model_Access_Restore_Missing assertions.
Trace the denied rejoin flow, recovery handshake flow, and post-recovery model access flow for the same canonical runtime client id.
Repair production behavior so continuity validation closes the recovery gate and Security Center no longer projects post-recovery trust as DIVERGED for the recovered client.
Preserve the pre-recovery denial frame: UNTRUSTED trust state and 423 denial must remain observable before missing-gap verification.
Preserve separate pre_recovery_console_status and post_recovery_console_status observations rather than reusing a mutable final state.
tasks.completionSignal: tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py::test_lease_expiry_blocks_untrusted_rejoin_until_gap_sync passes unchanged and still proves denied rejoin before recovery plus restored model access only after continuity validation.

tasks.title: Reprove full explicit gate
tasks.objective: Demonstrate that all repository-declared explicit testcases pass together in one evidence run.
steps: Rerun the exact full explicit pytest command recorded in design/KG/test-failure-records.json.
Require 9 passed in one run; partial success is not delivery acceptance.
If failures remain, refresh design/KG/test-failure-records.json with the new command, exit code, failing node, business category, and first actionable failure message.
Do not mark failures xfail or skip and do not remove any entrypoint from the command.
tasks.completionSignal: The full explicit pytest command exits 0 with all 9 entrypoints passed, and design/KG/test-failure-records.json contains no stale unresolved failure for a now-passing explicit testcase.

openGaps: The active .venv is missing pytest async plugin support, so tests/unit/routers/test_settings.py::test_put_then_get_roundtrip and tests/unit/routers/test_git.py::test_git_helper_uses_shared_command_runner currently fail before business assertions execute.

tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py::test_lease_expiry_blocks_untrusted_rejoin_until_gap_sync still reports category="Lease_Expiry_Active_Defense_Gap" because the distinct recovery control point and post-recovery model-access restoration are not yet contract-ready.

design/KG/SystemArchitecture.json models only the 4 security explicit testcases; the 5 CLI/backend unit explicit entrypoints are declared by tests/ARCHITECTURE.md and OVERALL_ARCHITECTURE.md, so they are handed to Coding/Repair through design/KG/test-failure-records.json, codingTargets, and taskExecutionPlan rather than schema-validated explicitEntrypoints.
